name: list_slow_queries
description:
<use_case>
      Use this tool to list slow queries from your Neon database.
    </use_case>

    <important_notes>
      This tool queries the pg_stat_statements extension to find queries that are taking longer than expected.
      The tool will return queries sorted by execution time, with the slowest queries first.
    </important_notes>

<notice>
Notice: Write mode active. Destructive tools are exposed. For tools with `destructiveHint: true`, NEVER invoke autonomously; always ask the user first.
</notice>

projectId.description: The ID of the project to list slow queries from

branchId.description: An optional ID of the branch. If not provided the default branch is used.

databaseName.description: The name of the database. If not provided, the default neondb or first available database is used.

computeId.description: The ID of the compute/endpoint. If not provided, the read-write compute associated with the branch will be used.

limit.description: Maximum number of slow queries to return

minExecutionTime.description: Minimum execution time in milliseconds to consider a query as slow
